counter_argument: Triggering the honest-client-desync path requires a client engine that has ALREADY diverged (a separate determinism bug) or a deliberately tampered client; with all-honest in-sync clients the reported cursor always equals the deterministic successor, so the gate is correct in the common case. Stakes are an anonymous casual artillery game with no auth/PII/money (ADR 0006), and impact is bounded to the attacker's own room. So it is a latent structural SPOF, not a live exploit.
rationale: PRIMARY of group referee-cursor-trust. The referee runs no physics and has no independent turn-order source of truth — it stores the submitter's reported nextActiveIndex verbatim as the gate for every later turn. The proper fix (authoritative referee that replays the shared engine over the log, per SPEC §5) is an ADR-grade architecture choice, not a mechanical patch; the cheap hardening (reject a reported seat that is dead/out-of-range/non-successor) only narrows it. Routed to decision-required so the thin-vs-authoritative-referee tradeoff is decided deliberately.

counter_argument: A member can already join and grief a room they are in (no-auth posture, ADR 0006); pointing the referee cursor at another seat in your OWN room is within that already-accepted trust boundary and has no cross-room/cross-tenant reach. Honest clients recompute the real successor, so for an all-honest table the rendered game is unaffected. The marginal harm over the accepted baseline is low for a casual game.
rationale: Security framing of the same root as reliability-003: the referee's only authz gate (active seat == acting seat) rests on peer-influenced state. Combined into group referee-cursor-trust; same remediation. ADR-grade response → decision-required.

counter_argument: Effect is referee-accepted out-of-phase buy rows scoped to the attacker's own room; honest engines route/ignore an out-of-phase buy deterministically so the game outcome is unaffected (log noise only). Replay-safe and within the accepted no-auth posture.
rationale: Same root as the group: the ROUND_OVER buy regime is gated only by the client-asserted roundOver flag because the referee derives no phase from the log. Folds into referee-cursor-trust; resolved by the same authoritative-vs-thin referee decision.

counter_argument: Bounded to the caller's OWN room's durable record; sanitizeScoreboard already range/shape-checks; no in-game state or cross-room impact, consistent with ADR 0006. Prior backlog #9 added membership+roster-range checks — this is the residual log-verification gap, not a regression.
rationale: Real, concrete forgeable durable record: finish_game writes match_scores/winner from the request body with no cross-check against the action log. Clears the low bar (conf 0.75 + concrete remediation: derive winner from log or explicitly document as advisory).

counter_argument: Not XSS (sink is CSSOM/canvas which ignore invalid values); impact is payload bloat in the shared room row. Bounded to rooms the member is in.
rationale: Concrete and cheap: color is validated only for non-empty string in all three write paths (name is capped at 20, color is not). Unbounded string persisted to shared JSONB and broadcast to peers. Clears low bar; effort S. Fix in the same shared validator the name uses.

counter_argument: AudioEngine is a page singleton (not a per-game leak); the AudioContext/listeners persisting for the session is by design. Only the stale doc reference + mid-burn napalm node leak at teardown are real.
rationale: Concrete maintenance trap + minor leak: napalmStart() doc references a reset() that does not exist, and sustained napalm source/gain nodes are not torn down if a game ends mid-burn. Clears low bar; effort S.

counter_argument: Fail-open is the correct liveness choice; the gap is only observability of the fail-open path. Overlaps the observability lens but is a distinct resilience concern.
rationale: Concrete: enforceRateLimit fails open on any RPC error with only a console.error, so a chronically-degraded limiter (missing table, perms regression) silently disables ALL per-IP limiting unnoticed. Remediation: distinguishable structured log/metric on the fail-open path (+ optional circuit breaker). Clears low bar.

POSITIVE CONTROL, not a defect: the only Math.random in the client is network backoff jitter, correctly OUTSIDE shared/ and never in the replayed path, so it cannot affect lockstep determinism. Recorded as evidence the invariant holds; nothing to fix. (The preventive CI-guard action it implies is captured by reliability-009.)

counter_argument: Requires a black-holed (hung, not erroring) connection during fire-recovery specifically; an erroring fetch is handled. But the FIRE_TIMEOUT watchdog fires once then defers to an unbounded fetch, so the stuck-lock it exists to prevent can still occur.
rationale: Concrete and real: no fetch in client/src uses an AbortController/timeout (grep-confirmed), so a hung resyncLog in recoverStuckFire never resolves and the player is trapped in 'Sending…' with no recovery but reload. Remediation: bound supabase reads / edge POSTs with an AbortController timeout and treat resync-overrun as failure.

counter_argument: The 10k-tick cap reportedly cannot be hit given the strictly-converging settle/flight models (see reliability-007), so the wedge is a defensive edge case, not an observed live freeze. Likelihood is low; impact (silent permanent per-client freeze for the match) is high.
rationale: Real recovery gap: on hitting the tick cap, tickToCompletion only console.errors and returns with the engine left in FIRING/RESOLVING, after which flushPendingActions never drains again — a silent permanent freeze with no UI signal. Remediation: force a recoverable phase or surface a fatal-desync banner on cap.

counter_argument: observed:false — the agent itself notes settleStep currently cannot fail to converge (strictly-downward sand model), so this is a speculative defensive-parity suggestion, not a demonstrated defect.
rationale: Below the calibrated confidence gate after discounting the inferred/speculative basis. Routed to investigate (defensive RESOLVING-phase iteration cap) rather than filed; folds naturally into reliability-006's recovery work if picked up.

counter_argument: Not a desync (fire still applies in seq order, engine phase-gates correctly); effect is a brief HUD flicker + the per-shot watchdog being defeated for a one-seq window. Genuinely minor.
rationale: Real but below the low-severity bar (needs conf >=0.75 for a low to be issue-eligible; calibrated to 0.65). Routed to investigate. If the firing-lock work in reliability-005/006 is picked up, fix this together (key the lock release on the local submit's own seq).

counter_argument: It is a preventive control, not a current defect — the invariant already holds. But preventing silent regression of the prime invariant is high-value and cheap.
rationale: Actionable preventive: add a CI/lint guard (or scripts/checks harness) that fails the build if Date.now/performance.now/Math.random/new Date appears under shared/src/engine. Protects lockstep determinism from silent regression. Clears low bar; effort S.

counter_argument: Logs are Supabase-dashboard-access-gated; no secret/key is exposed, only Postgres internals (codes, constraint/column names). Low real exposure.
rationale: Concrete batch fix across ~10 functions: log error.message (the pattern already correct in _shared/mod.ts:91) instead of the full Supabase error object. Clears low bar; effort S.

counter_argument: Modulo bias is cosmetic (negligible A–D overweight). Enumeration is throttled by the 20/min join_room limit and rooms are ephemeral with no PII/stakes; only distributed/IP-rotating brute force is practical.
rationale: The actionable core is the small 36^4 code space for private rooms. Remediation: 6-char codes (36^6) or a per-room failed-join lockout (+ optional rejection-sampling to remove bias). Clears low bar; effort S.

counter_argument: Current tree is clean (no real .env committed) and the global .env catch-all already ignores the file; this is belt-and-suspenders only.
rationale: Cheap defense-in-depth around the file that holds the service-role key: add explicit 'supabase/functions/.env' and '.env.local' entries to .gitignore. Clears low bar; effort S.

counter_argument: No PII/auth tokens in the app and no confirmed XSS, so the practical exposure (clickjacking, MIME-sniff) is modest for a casual game.
rationale: Concrete, conventional hardening: add X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, Referrer-Policy (and ideally a CSP) to nginx.conf and a netlify.toml [[headers]] block. Clears low bar; effort S.

counter_argument: Same major version, backward-compatible API; currently resolves to the identical 2.107.0. Operational drift risk, not a security defect today.
rationale: Below the low-severity issue bar (conf 0.7 < 0.75). Routed to investigate: pin client @supabase/supabase-js to match the esm.sh edge pin, or document the manual sync step.

counter_argument: esm.sh with an exact version tag is low mutation-risk and Supabase caches modules; the gap is only integrity-hash pinning, an industry best-practice rather than a live vuln.
rationale: Concrete supply-chain hardening: commit a deno.lock capturing integrity hashes for the esm.sh import (or vendor supabase-js locally), and update the .gitignore comment. Clears low bar; effort M.
